Instruments working to place suture through the Penrose drain at marked locations

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good technique for surgeon's knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Continuing first throw formation with proper hand positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Maintaining good control of suture ends

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw tightening, surgeon's knot appears well formed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension control during knot tightening

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with hand switching, single throw formation
Working on third and final throw with appropriate hand switching
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent technique maintained through all throws

frame_analyses.description: Final knot secured, preparing to cut suture ends with scissors
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears well secured before cutting

frame_analyses.description: Task completed, both suture ends cut, drain appears properly closed

knot_assessments.notes: Well-executed surgeon's knot with proper double wrap
Proper single throw with correct hand switching
Final throw completed with appropriate hand switching

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Competent performance with proper surgeon's knot formation and correct hand switching between throws. Suture placement appears accurate with good drain closure.
improvement_suggestions: Work on slightly faster transitions between phases
Continue practicing consistent suture placement accuracy
strengths: Clean final result with complete drain closure
